name: Stir Fried Vegetables with Bean Curd
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Cup giant bamboo shoots, cut into sticks
recipeInstructions:
1. Preparation: Cut pressed beancurd into 2-inch sticks (the size of a lead pencil). Wash and trim celery and carrots; trim strings from the back of celery; cut into sticks to match pressed beancurd. Wash and slice giant bamboo shoots across grain to match beancurd sticks. Peel onion, remove layers; cut into sticks.
2. Combine chicken stock, salt, sugar, ginger, sherry, and sesame oil in a bowl. Reserve.
3. Add carrots to rapidly boiling water for 15 seconds. Then add celery for another 15 seconds. Drain vegetables and plunge them into running cold water to stop the cooking process. Drain and reserve.
4. Stir-frying: Heat wok to very hot. Add oil. Let oil heat for a few seconds, then add onions; toss for 10 seconds. Add celery, carrots, and bamboo shoots; toss for 1 minute. Slowly pour in chicken stock mixture around the sides of the pan so it will heat quickly. When liquid boils, add beancurd sticks, taking care not to break pieces. Thicken liquid slightly with a dribble or two of cornstarch paste. Keep stirring gently to reduce liquid. Remove to serving platter.
